title: Surface errors as exceptions, never suppress them
description: Throw and catch typed exceptions rather than relying on warnings, false returns, or the @ operator.
body: Use when handling failure in any PHP path. Do not use the @ error-suppression operator, ignore a function's false return, or wrap code in an empty catch. Actions: convert recoverable failures into typed exceptions, catch the narrowest exception class you can handle, let unrecoverable ones propagate to a central handler, and configure the runtime to throw on errors where the framework supports it. Validation signals include a test asserting the specific exception type is thrown and that no path swallows it silently. Risk boundary: a suppressed error becomes a silent data-corruption or security bug; an empty catch or @ on a security-relevant call is a blocking finding to remove.
